MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Mark Hoosmand, 415.472.5700 of Hoosmand Law Gropu for Defendants Loi Truong et al. appearing via Zoom.
No other appearancs.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LOI TRUONG, KEITH ARMSTRONG, MY NGUYEN, SUMMER SIMPSON MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. While not entirely clear, complaint appears to allege a cause of action for breach of contract (From Complaint PLD-C-001 CONTRACT) seeking to recover for damages to Plaintiff's property and for discrimination (Complaint for Damages 5: 11-13). Moving party seeks to strike the entire complaint arguing that the gravamen of Plaintiffs' relevant causes of action arise from Defendants' statements and participation in the prior lawsuit. While the complaint refences hiring lawyers, contacting DBI, lying 3 times in different courts, moving party does not identify any claims that arise out of these references. The Complaint seeks recovery for damage to property, discrimination and emotional distress and alleges in bold that defendants "vandalized mailbox, smoked in the room, destroyed and yanked bathtub, deliberately waste water, yelled "Chines Dog, Go Back to China" as form of discrimination, etc." Moving party failed to carry its burden and show that all or any claims in the complaint arise out of protected activity relying on the inapplicable "gravamen" of the complaint analysis. "At the first step, the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 396. Moving party failed to do so. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
